text.title: AI-Assisted Market Sizing Method
text.summary: Top-down and bottom-up triangulation rules, tool table, and required checklist for AI-assisted TAM/SAM/SOM estimation.

rule.statement: Run top-down and bottom-up methods in separate agent calls; do not share intermediate results between paths before triangulation.
rule.rationale: If the two paths share data during computation, they are not independent. Non-independent paths produce false convergence and inflate confidence beyond what the evidence supports.

rule.statement: If both methods agree within 2x, report the median with a confidence range (low/mid/high). If they diverge beyond 2x, report both values and flag the assumption driving the gap — do not average.
rule.rationale: Averaging divergent estimates creates false precision. The gap itself is a signal: it identifies the key unknown that needs primary-source resolution before the estimate is defensible.

example.code:
Top-Down: Market reports → industry size → segment → target $100B market → 20% segment → 5% addressable = $1B TAM
Bottom-Up: # potential customers × average revenue = TAM 100K companies × $10K/year = $1B
Triangulate: both ≈ $1B → confidence HIGH, report $1B mid with $800M-$1.2B range
example.note: Both paths yield similar numbers; the estimate is defensible and can be reported.

list.item: Statista AI — market reports, data (export only, no API)
CB Insights — company data, trends (CSV export, useful for bottom-up)
Perplexity Pro — synthesis from multiple sources (REST API)
AlphaSense — industry research, SEC filings (partial API)

rule.statement: Before publishing any market size estimate, verify all checklist items are complete.
rule.rationale: Skipping any item — especially assumption documentation or confidence ranges — creates a false-precision artifact that reviewers cannot challenge or reproduce.

list.item: Market boundaries defined clearly (geography, customer type, minimum deal size)
Multiple data sources used (minimum 2 independent sources per method)
Both bottom-up and top-down estimates produced independently
Every assumption documented (one line per assumption in the output)
Confidence ranges included (low/mid/high), not point estimates
Data recency verified — flag sources older than 12 months

antipattern.description: AI tools confuse TAM, SAM, and SOM if not defined explicitly in the prompt.
antipattern.reason: LLMs use these terms interchangeably in training data; without explicit definitions in the prompt, output will conflate them and produce inconsistent estimates.

antipattern.description: Currency and geography scope creep: AI frequently extends a US-market figure to global without flagging the assumption.
antipattern.reason: Geographic scope must be declared in the prompt as a hard constraint, not inferred, to prevent silent extrapolation.
